summary: DHH draws on Matt Taibbi's thesis that the political left has adopted tactics once associated with the right, particularly censorship and moral panic. He focuses on the campaign to remove Joe Rogan from Spotify over his interview with vaccine skeptic Dr. Robert Malone, arguing it mirrors the conservative moral panics of the 80s and 90s. DHH contends that science requires adversarial debate and that platforming controversial voices is a tradition of great interviewers. He notes the irony that the same liberals who mocked conservative censorship campaigns now deploy identical tactics, and predicts the outrage will only boost Rogan's audience, just as moral panics historically amplified what they sought to suppress.
key_points: The political left has adopted censorship tactics (deplatforming, moral outrage) that were once hallmarks of the religious right in the 80s and 90s
Science depends on adversarial debate, and shutting down dissenting voices undermines the scientific process rather than protecting it
Rogan's long-form interview style follows a legitimate tradition of interviewers like William Buckley who let intellectual adversaries present their arguments
Outrage campaigns against content creators function as free advertising, just as moral panics boosted Eminem, NWA, and Dungeons & Dragons
There are signs of pushback against this trend, such as McWhorter's mainstream acceptance and the Virginia political upset
money_quotes: What is science, if not an adversarial process by which we seek an ever-closer understanding of the truth through critical review from all sides?
How can we follow The Science, if we do not subject it to such pressures?
It was not only coming from the other side, but it was laughed at by liberals and progressives as preposterous.
Things have shifted. Drastically and quickly.
I can't help but chuckle at how this outrage is performing the same function for Rogan that picketing parents did for Eminem: MAKE A LOT OF PEOPLE WANT TO LISTEN!
The more you tell people how dangerous this music/game/interview is, the more appealing it'll appear to many. It's basic human nature!
key_insight: The political left has become the new moral majority, deploying the same censorship tactics it once ridiculed the right for using, and with the same counterproductive results.
